verses.text: Nahash the Ammonite besieges Jabesh-gilead and offers terms of pure cruelty — surrender, and lose your right eyes as a public shame. The world's mercy is no mercy. Into this hopeless corner God will send deliverance through the king He has just given Israel.

Nahash the Ammonite besieges Jabesh-gilead and offers terms of pure cruelty — surrender, and lose your right eyes as a public shame. The world's mercy is no mercy. Into this hopeless corner God will send deliverance through the king He has just given Israel.

Nahash the Ammonite besieges Jabesh-gilead and offers terms of pure cruelty — surrender, and lose your right eyes as a public shame. The world's mercy is no mercy. Into this hopeless corner God will send deliverance through the king He has just given Israel.

When Saul hears the news, 'the Spirit of God came upon Saul, and his anger was kindled greatly.' Here is anger of the right kind — roused against cruelty and for God's people. The same Spirit that gifts us for service can stir a holy indignation at evil.

When Saul hears the news, 'the Spirit of God came upon Saul, and his anger was kindled greatly.' Here is anger of the right kind — roused against cruelty and for God's people. The same Spirit that gifts us for service can stir a holy indignation at evil.

When Saul hears the news, 'the Spirit of God came upon Saul, and his anger was kindled greatly.' Here is anger of the right kind — roused against cruelty and for God's people. The same Spirit that gifts us for service can stir a holy indignation at evil.

Saul cuts a yoke of oxen in pieces and sends them through Israel: come out, or so shall your oxen be. The summons works; a great army gathers as one man. A leader filled with God's Spirit can rouse a scattered people to act together.

Saul cuts a yoke of oxen in pieces and sends them through Israel: come out, or so shall your oxen be. The summons works; a great army gathers as one man. A leader filled with God's Spirit can rouse a scattered people to act together.

Saul divides the host and falls on the Ammonites at dawn, scattering them so that no two are left together. The city that faced shame and ruin is saved. When God raises a deliverer, the proud enemy that boasted yesterday is broken in a single morning.

Saul divides the host and falls on the Ammonites at dawn, scattering them so that no two are left together. The city that faced shame and ruin is saved. When God raises a deliverer, the proud enemy that boasted yesterday is broken in a single morning.

Saul divides the host and falls on the Ammonites at dawn, scattering them so that no two are left together. The city that faced shame and ruin is saved. When God raises a deliverer, the proud enemy that boasted yesterday is broken in a single morning.

The people now want to kill those who had earlier despised Saul. But he refuses: 'there shall not a man be put to death this day: for to day the LORD hath wrought salvation.' A day of victory is no time for revenge; he gives God the glory and spares his scorners.

The people now want to kill those who had earlier despised Saul. But he refuses: 'there shall not a man be put to death this day: for to day the LORD hath wrought salvation.' A day of victory is no time for revenge; he gives God the glory and spares his scorners.

Samuel leads the people to Gilgal to renew the kingdom before the LORD, and 'there they made Saul king... and rejoiced greatly.' What began in their sinful demand God folds into a day of worship and joy. He can redeem even our flawed beginnings for His purposes.
